body.title: HYPOTHESIZE prior repair from observed SCOPE_GUARD and old_string errors
body.result: added pe_chain_priors table + scope_guard/hypothesize hooks + top-5 prior injection into HYPOTHESIZE prompt; 10 tests
body.description:
pe_chain HYPOTHESIZE has been hallucinating thalamus.py as an edit target for 7+ weeks with no mechanism to update its priors. Add a pe_chain_priors table keyed on (target_path, symbol), append on every SCOPE_GUARD trip and every old_string mismatch, and inject top-N recent priors into HYPOTHESIZE's prompt context so the model stops regenerating known-bad targets.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py, wild_igor/igor/tools/scope_guard.py (write hook), instance schema (new table pe_chain_priors)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; Postgres only; upsert semantics on (target_path, symbol); prior injection is prompt-side only, no model weights
**Scope boundary:** in scope: pe_chain_priors table + migration, append on SCOPE_GUARD trip and old_string mismatch, inject top-5 negative priors into HYPOTHESIZE prompt. Out of scope: retraining any model, RL, changing SCOPE_GUARD allowlist logic, tools other than pe_chain.
**Test plan:** test SCOPE_GUARD trip writes to pe_chain_priors; test HYPOTHESIZE prompt includes prior entries when they exist; integration test that thalamus.py as target is suppressed when it appears in priors.
